zotero.abstractNote: World models are essential for intelligent agents to predict, plan, and reason. While hierarchical world models promise to capture long-term dependencies by operating at different temporal scales, they often struggle with the trade-off between temporal abstraction and predictive accuracy. We propose Thick World Models (TWM), a hierarchical world model that learns adaptive temporal abstractions from discrete latent dynamics. TWM employs a hierarchy of discrete latent spaces, where higher levels operate at slower temporal resolutions. We introduce a novel objective that encourages the model to learn temporal abstractions by predicting future states at multiple time scales simultaneously. Our experiments demonstrate that TWM outperforms state-of-the-art hierarchical world models on complex control tasks, showing improved sample efficiency and the ability to learn meaningful temporal abstractions.
zotero.conferenceName: The Twelfth International Conference on Learning Representations
zotero.proceedingsTitle: The Twelfth International Conference on Learning Representations
